channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: HUD chief showcases how private-public partnerships uplift economically neglected areas in Philly tour
item.description: HUD Secretary Scott Turner visited Philadelphia on Thursday to see how Opportunity Zones and private investments have revitalized areas of the city that had long been neglected.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FCC chair opens investigation into Disney and ABC over DEI practices
item.description: FCC chair Brendan Carr said he wants a review all of Disney's DEI practices — both past and present — to determine whether the company has ever violated any FCC regulations.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim, MPR News

item.title: AG files last-minute appeal to block Musk from gifting $2 million to voters in high-stakes WI court race
item.description: Waukesha County Judge and former Wisconsin Attorney General Brad Schimel faces liberal Judge Susan Crawford on Tuesday, and a new filing targets Elon Musk.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he's considering ways to serve a third term as president
item.description: President Donald Trump says he's considering ways to serve a third term. “I'm not joking,” he says in an NBC News interview.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin's attorney general asks the state Supreme Court to stop Musk's $1 million payments
item.description: Wisconsin’s attorney general has asked the liberal-controlled state Supreme Court to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters. The request comes hours before President Donald Trump’s ally plans the giveaway at an evening rally in Green Bay.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump admin's 'sloppy' Signal leak put Democrats back on offense, Sen Warner says
item.description: Sen. Mark Warner, D-Va., says the Pentagon's "sloppy" handling of its Signal chat has put Democrats back on offense.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he’s ‘very angry’ and ‘pissed off’ at Putin during NBC News interview
item.description: President Donald Trump said he was “very angry” and “pissed off” when Russian President Vladimir Putin criticized the credibility of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy’s leadership, adding that the comments were “not going in the right location.
item.dc:creator: Kristin Welker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota union employees demand Walz halt half-time in office policy
item.description: Around 500 Minnesota state employees protested Saturday, calling on DFL Gov. Tim Walz to reverse course on a policy to require more in-office work. The governor says having more employees spend at least half their time at their workplaces will improve communication and collaboration.
item.dc:creator: MPR News Staff, MPR News

item.title: Trump roars down multiple paths of retribution as he vowed. Some targets yield while others fight
item.description: In the early part of his second term, President Donald Trump has taken action against individuals, law firms, private corporations, media companies and universities he regards as adversaries.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Calvin Woodward, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Schumer, Democrats seek to get off the mat amid new Trump controversy
item.description: Democrats and their embattled Senate Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) have received a welcome change to rebound amid the controversy surrounding the Signal group chat that entangled a number of Trump Cabinet members over the past week.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats ramp up town halls in GOP districts
item.description: House Democrats are ramping up their aggressive strategy of conducting town halls in Republican-held districts, vying to exploit the GOP’s advised moratorium on the events to make inroads with frustrated voters, pick up battleground seats, and flip control of the House in next year’s midterms.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: University of Minnesota leaders say ICE has detained a graduate student
item.description: The University of Minnesota says one of its graduate students is being detained by U.S. immigration authorities.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: American woman held in Afghanistan by the Taliban has been released, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An American woman detained for weeks in Afghanistan by the Taliban has been released from custody, according to a person familiar with the matter and a social media post Saturday from a longtime U.S. diplomat.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A weekend ritual for Trump's Florida die-hards to get a brief glimpse of their political hero
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — The sun was shining outside President Donald Trump's West Palm Beach golf course on Saturday morning when Alan Mentser got a call letting him know that police were shutting down a road nearby. It was almost time to “show the boss a little love.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Most US Institute of Peace workers get late-night word of their mass firing
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most employees at the U.S. Institute of Peace, a congressionally created and funded think tank now taken over by Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency, received email notices of their mass firing, the latest step in the Trump administration's government downsizing.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump appoints Sara Carter as next drug czar, says she 'will lead the charge to protect' US
item.description: President Donald Trump appointed Sara Carter as drug czar, saying she will "lead the charge to protect our nation and save our children from the scourge of drugs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Meet the DOGE team
item.description: Several members of Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) made their first public appearance. Here's who they are.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Dan Goldman supports reforms to NY's evidence laws
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York State budget deadline is this upcoming Tuesday. One of Gov. Kathy Hochul’s priorities for this session is to reform New York’s evidence laws, which were passed in 2019. The governor’s proposal includes three major provisions.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Illinois to defy Trump voter order for April election
item.description: President Trump earlier this week issued an executive order calling for all voters to provide proof of citizenship before receiving a ballot.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WGN 9

item.title: Elon Musk to hand out $1M checks ahead of judicial race in Wisconsin
item.description: Elon Musk said he would be traveling to Wisconsin before the Supreme Court race to speak with voters and give out $1 million checks to several people who have already cast ballots.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Florida considers bill to roll back child labor laws to fill jobs once held by undocumented migrants
item.description: Florida lawmakers are considering major changes to state child labor laws to address a potential shortage of migrant workers due to GOP-led immigration crackdowns, which would allow teens to work longer hours and earn lower wages.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, PIX 11

item.title: Is the fight over? Florida bill to allow guns on campus gets shot down
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (WFLA) — A rare rejection happened at the state capitol as a Republican-led bill was voted down by a fellow GOP lawmaker. Senate Bill 814 would have allowed concealed carry on college campuses, but it died in its first committee stop.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Appeals court won't lift Boasberg's order blocking Alien Enemies Act
item.description: A federal appeals court in a 2-1 decision Wednesday refused to lift U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s order blocking the Trump administration from swiftly deporting migrants under the Alien Enemies Act. The Justice Department had urged the three-judge panel on the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: California lawmaker revives effort to end forced prison labor
item.description: A new California bill seeks to prohibit the Department of Corrections and Rehabilitation from forcing inmates to work.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Trump portrait to be removed from Colorado Capitol after president's criticism
item.description: President Trump has criticized a portrait of himself hanging in the Colorado Capitol, suggesting it was intentionally distorted, while Republican lawmakers have asked for it to be removed.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Live updates: Ratcliffe, Gabbard pressed on war plans Signal group chat
item.description: The Trump administration is scrambling to contain the fallout from a bombshell Atlantic article revealing its editor-in-chief had been mistakenly included in a Signal group chat involving national security adviser Mike Waltz, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, intelligence leadership and other Trump ad
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5
